display.purpose: Place names in hero cards, greeting headlines. One per section maximum.

headline.purpose: Section headers and featured card titles.

title.purpose: Card names in horizontal scroll, dialog headings.

body.purpose: Descriptions, metadata, community text. 65-75ch max on desktop.

label.purpose: Navigation, badge text, price chips, filter pills.

shadows.name: glass-depth
shadows.purpose: Glass cards floating over photography only. Not for flat content cards.

shadows.name: mint-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary CTA button and center FAB only. Communicates the single action on screen.

motion.name: pulse-soft
motion.purpose: FAB glow breathing. One instance per screen. Defined in tailwind.config.js.

breakpoints.name: mobile-max
breakpoints.purpose: Primary design target. All mobile layouts designed at 390-430px.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar appears. Bottom nav hides. Content centers with max-w-[430px] phone frame.

components.name: Primary CTA Button (Mint)
components.description: Full-pill, Oasis Spring background. The single action on any given surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-mint { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; background: #7CF7C8; color: #050B1E; border: none; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 10px 20px; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px -6px rgba(124,247,200,0.45); transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,0,0.1,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,0,0.1,1); } .ds-btn-mint:hover { background: #1EC99A; transform: scale(1.02); } .ds-btn-mint:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-mint:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7CF7C8; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action on dark backgrounds. Glass surface, Desert Moon text.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 6px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: #B8C2D6; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.14); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(124,247,200,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Category Filter Pill
components.description: Horizontal-scroll filter row. Active state in Oasis Spring; inactive in glass.
components.css: .ds-filter-row { display: flex; gap: 8px; overflow-x: auto; padding: 8px 16px; scrollbar-width: none; } .ds-filter-row::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; } .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; flex-shrink: 0; padding: 8px 14px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); color: #B8C2D6; transition: background 150ms, color 150ms, border-color 150ms; } .ds-pill:hover { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-color: rgba(124,247,200,0.40); } .ds-pill--active { background: #7CF7C8; color: #050B1E; border-color: transparent; box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -3px rgba(124,247,200,0.45); }

components.name: Content Card (Place / Tour / Rental)
components.description: Horizontal-scroll item. Image-first with rating badge, type tag, title, metadata.
components.html:
4.9Heritage

Al-Turaif District

Riyadh
components.css: .ds-content-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex-shrink: 0; width: 176px; background: #081229; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 1.25rem; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,0,0.1,1); } .ds-content-card:active { transform: scale(0.96); } .ds-content-card__img { position: relative; height: 112px; background: #101B36; } .ds-rating-badge { position: absolute; top: 8px; right: 8px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 3px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.55); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 3px 8px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; } .ds-type-tag { position: absolute; bottom: 8px; left: 8px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.55); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 3px 8px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; } .ds-content-card__body { padding: 12px; } .ds-content-card__name { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; line-height: 1.35; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-content-card__meta { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 400; color: #7F8AA3; margin: 0; }

components.name: Rating Badge
components.description: Absolute-positioned overlay on card images. The only gold foreground element.
components.html: 4.9
components.css: .ds-rating-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.55); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 4px 10px; } .ds-rating-pill span { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: #fff; }

components.name: Bottom Navigation Bar
components.description: Fixed bottom bar. 2 items left, center FAB elevated above bar, 2 items right.
components.html: Quick Plan
components.css: .ds-bottom-nav { position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; background: rgba(8,18,41,0.92); backdrop-filter: blur(16px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(16px); border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); padding-bottom: max(env(safe-area-inset-bottom), 8px); z-index: 40; } .ds-bottom-nav > * { display: flex; align-items: flex-end; justify-content: space-around; padding: 8px 8px 0; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 2px; flex: 1; padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 1rem; border: none; background: transparent; color: #7F8AA3; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms, background 150ms; } .ds-nav-item span { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-nav-item--active { color: #7CF7C8; background: rgba(124,247,200,0.10); } .ds-fab-wrapper { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: -20px; } .ds-fab { width: 56px; height: 56px; border-radius: 9999px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7CF7C8, #1EC99A); border: 4px solid #081229; color: #050B1E; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px -6px rgba(124,247,200,0.5); transition: transform 150ms; } .ds-fab:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } .ds-fab:active { transform: scale(0.90); } .ds-fab-label { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #7CF7C8; margin-top: 2px; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Search Bar
components.description: Full-width tap target. Opens global search overlay on tap, no inline editing.
components.css: .ds-search-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; width: 100%; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); border-radius: 9999px; padding: 12px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms, transform 100ms; } .ds-search-bar:active { transform: scale(0.99); } .ds-search-bar__placeholder { flex: 1; text-align: left; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #7F8AA3; } .ds-search-bar__icon { width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #7CF7C8; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: #050B1E; flex-shrink: 0; transition: background 150ms; } .ds-search-bar:hover .ds-search-bar__icon { background: #1EC99A; }

narrative.overview:
Tripo is used most often in the evening. A Saudi resident settling into their sofa, phone in hand, deciding where to go this weekend or planning a group escape. The surface needs to feel like a knowledgeable local friend in a warmly-lit room, not a marketplace, not a travel agency, not a dashboard. Deep, quiet backgrounds with precise, luminous accents. The darkness amplifies desire; the city photos pop against navy the same way a destination looks better at night.

The system runs on a full palette: mint for motion and intent, gold for earned value and reputation, coral for urgency and warnings, all on a foundation of three navy depths that create spatial hierarchy without a single shadow. Glassmorphism appears only when a card genuinely floats over imagery; it is never decorative layering. Typography is a single variable-weight sans at extreme weights, achieving contrast through compression: 900 for names and CTAs, 400 for context.

This system explicitly rejects: transactional white-card grids with price-first layouts, Airbnb-clone aesthetics with rental-listing sameness, any 'dark mode because cool' justification, and the neon-on-black nightlife aesthetic. Tripo is premium travel, not a nightclub app.
keyCharacteristics: Navy foundation with three elevation steps (950/900/800), no shadows at rest
Single sans (Inter), heavy weight contrast (900/700/400), no decorative serifs
Mint is the one action color, reserved for CTAs, active states, and confirmation
Gold appears only in reputation contexts: ratings, Karam tier, earned rewards
Glass surfaces are conditional: only when overlaying photography or deep backgrounds
RTL (Arabic) and LTR (English) are equal first-class design states

rules.name: The One Mint Rule
rules.body: Oasis Spring appears as a CTA, active state, or brand mark only. It is never used as a decorative background, section divider, or general accent. Every screen has one action, one mint.

rules.name: The Gold Gating Rule
rules.body: Karam Gold is the color of reputation, not decoration. If an element doesn't represent a rating, tier, or earned achievement, it cannot use gold.

rules.name: The Glass Gate Rule
rules.body: backdrop-filter blur is permitted only when a card surface sits directly over a photograph or the Midnight Interior background. Cards on white or Lifted Surface never blur. One glass layer per scroll depth maximum.

rules.name: The Weight Wall Rule
rules.body: The minimum weight contrast between a headline and its supporting text is 200. Never two consecutive elements at the same weight. The eye must know immediately what to read first.

rules.name: The Scale Floor Rule
rules.body: Nothing below 9px renders in production. Running body text never drops below 14px.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Cards, list items, and containers have no shadow at rest. Tonal background contrast is the depth signal. A shadow appears only on the active CTA (mint glow) or a glass card floating over photography.

rules.name: The No Nesting Rule
rules.body: Glass cards never contain content cards. Content cards never contain cards. One depth signal per container.

dos: Do lead every card with the destination's photograph or imagery. Text and price follow.
Do use Oasis Spring as the single CTA color across any given screen. One action, one mint.
Do use the three navy depths (950/900/800) to create spatial hierarchy without adding shadows.
Do show place names in Arabic and English where both are available.
Do use Karam Gold only for ratings, tier badges, and earned-value indicators.
Do keep Inter at 900 or 700 for all heading and interactive label text.
Do apply glass cards exclusively over photography or the deepest navy backgrounds.
Do validate that touch targets are ≥44px on all interactive elements.
donts: Don't use price as the primary information hierarchy on any card.
Don't use background-clip text with a gradient. All text is a single color.
Don't apply backdrop-filter blur to a card sitting on a white or light-mode surface.
Don't use glass cards as a general decorative treatment.
Don't place Waypoint Red on more than one element per screen.
Don't use identical same-sized card grids with the same icon-heading-text structure repeated.
Don't design components in LTR and mirror them for RTL as an afterthought.
Don't let any feature push a Booking.com or Airbnb-clone information density.
Don't use Karam Gold decoratively outside rating and earned-value contexts.
Don't apply the pulse-soft animation to anything other than the center FAB.
